sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.46 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: A finite group$G$is said to be almost simple\/ if$T G { Aut}(T)$for some nonabelian simple group$T$. By definition, a finite linear space\/ consists of a set$V$of points, together with a collection of$k$-element subsets of$V$, called lines\/ ($k 3$), such that every pair of points is contained in exactly one line. Classify the finite linear spaces which admit a line-transitive almost simple subgro...
